title: Contributing to Sofamatic
text: Thanks interest improving Sofamatic. Bug reports, feature requests, pull requests welcome.

title: Contributing to Sofamatic
text: Please open issue using Bug report template include self-contained reprex plus output sessionInfo().

title: Contributing to Sofamatic
text: Open issue using Feature request template, describing clinical / analytic motivation well proposed API.

title: Contributing to Sofamatic
text: Fork repository create feature branch main. Make changes. Keep functions vectorised dependency-light (base R , reasonable). Document every exported function roxygen2 regenerate man pages NAMESPACE: Test. Add unit tests tests/testthat/ cover boundary cases new behaviour. Run: Check. package must pass errors warnings: Update NEWS.md user-facing change. Submit pull request main. CI run R-CMD-check macOS, Windows, Linux (release + devel + oldrel-1).
code: devtools::document() devtools::test() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to Sofamatic
text: Follow tidyverse style guide — two-space indents, snake_case function names. Keep arguments order-stable; prefer adding new arguments end sensible defaults. Avoid heavy dependencies; goal sofamatic install cleanly R install.

title: NA
text: Bug fix New feature Breaking change Documentation

title: NA
text: devtools::test() passes locally devtools::check() reports 0 errors / 0 warnings New behaviour covered tests NEWS.md updated user-facing changes Roxygen documentation regenerated (devtools::document())

title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: Generate slightly larger cohort README example: 60 subjects, 10 days , long format. Compute every sub-score, total SOFA_score, plus longitudinal helpers SOFA_admission SOFA_max single call. Three views ship door free: row-level sub-scores total (SOFA_resp … SOFA_score), per-subject admission score (SOFA_admission, constant within subject_id), per-subject worst score follow-(SOFA_max).
code: cohort <- example_sofa_data(n_subjects = 60, days_each = 10) dim(cohort) #> [1] 600  17 head(cohort, 3) #>   subject_id day age sex bmi pao2_fio2 ventilated platelets bilirubin #> 1          1   0  78   M  34      12.1          1        91        39 #> 2          1   1  78   M  34       9.4          1       215        28 #> 3          1   2  78   M  34      22.3          1       170        52 #>   vasopressors noradrenaline  map sedation gcs creatinine urine_output dialysis #> 1            1         0.034 52.9        1  NA        106         1177        0 #> 2            0         0.000 93.8        1   9         54          889        0 #> 3            0         0.000 74.6        1   3         20          163        0 scored <- calculate_sofa(cohort, id = "subject_id", time = "day")  scored[scored$subject_id == 1,        c("day", "SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",          "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal",          "SOFA_score", "SOFA_admission", "SOFA_max")] #>    day SOFA_resp SOFA_coag SOFA_liver SOFA_cardio SOFA_neuro SOFA_renal #> 1    0         4         2          2           3          2          0 #> 2    1         4         0          1           0          3          0 #> 3    2         3         0          2           0          4          4 #> 4    3         3         0          2           0          2          1 #> 5    4         0         0          0           0          4          4 #> 6    5         0         0          2           0          2          1 #> 7    6         2         2          2           0          2          2 #> 8    7         2         0          0           0          3          0 #> 9    8         3         0          1           3          4          0 #> 10   9         3         0          0           0          1          1 #>    SOFA_score SOFA_admission SOFA_max #> 1          13             13       13 #> 2           8             13       13 #> 3          13             13       13 #> 4           8             13       13 #> 5           8             13       13 #> 6           5             13       13 #> 7          10             13       13 #> 8           5             13       13 #> 9          11             13       13 #> 10          5             13       13

title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: organ failure look like day 0?  common cohort summary prevalence organ failure per system, defined sub-score ≥ 3:
code: admission <- scored[scored$day == 0, ]  barplot(table(factor(admission$SOFA_score,                      levels = 0:max(admission$SOFA_score))),         col  = "grey70", border = NA,         xlab = "SOFA score on admission",         ylab = "Number of patients",         main = "Distribution of admission SOFA") parts <- c("SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",            "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal")  prevalence <- sapply(parts, function(p) mean(admission[[p]] >= 3)) round(sort(prevalence, decreasing = TRUE), 2) #>   SOFA_resp SOFA_cardio  SOFA_neuro  SOFA_renal   SOFA_coag  SOFA_liver  #>        0.43        0.40        0.40        0.15        0.07        0.00

title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: Mean inter-quartile band SOFA_score day:  spaghetti plot individual trajectories shows heterogeneous cohort patient level:
code: by_day <- aggregate(SOFA_score ~ day, scored, FUN = function(x) {   c(mean = mean(x),     q25  = unname(quantile(x, 0.25)),     q75  = unname(quantile(x, 0.75))) }) by_day <- do.call(data.frame, by_day) names(by_day) <- c("day", "mean", "q25", "q75")  plot(by_day$day, by_day$mean, type = "n",      ylim = range(c(by_day$q25, by_day$q75)),      xlab = "Day since admission",      ylab = "SOFA score",      main = "Mean SOFA across the cohort (band = IQR)") polygon(c(by_day$day, rev(by_day$day)),         c(by_day$q25, rev(by_day$q75)),         col = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.2), border = NA) lines(by_day$day, by_day$mean, col = "steelblue", lwd = 2) points(by_day$day, by_day$mean, col = "steelblue", pch = 19) plot(NA, xlim = range(scored$day),      ylim = c(0, max(scored$SOFA_score)),      xlab = "Day since admission",      ylab = "SOFA score",      main = "Per-patient SOFA trajectories") for (sid in unique(scored$subject_id)) {   s <- scored[scored$subject_id == sid, ]   s <- s[order(s$day), ]   lines(s$day, s$SOFA_score,         col = adjustcolor("black", alpha.f = 0.25)) }

what: Which organ systems drive the score?
title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: Mean sub-score day, stacked:  synthetic cohort respiratory cardiovascular components dominate, consistent underlying generator calibrated (heavy ventilation prevalence, frequent vasopressor use).
code: mean_parts <- sapply(parts, function(p)   tapply(scored[[p]], scored$day, mean, na.rm = TRUE))  barplot(t(mean_parts),         col          = grey.colors(length(parts), start = 0.3, end = 0.9),         legend.text  = sub("SOFA_", "", parts),         args.legend  = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", cex = 0.8),         xlab         = "Day since admission",         ylab         = "Mean sub-score (stacked)",         main         = "Organ-system contributions to mean SOFA")

title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: Many studies define ICU deterioration SOFA_max − SOFA_admission ≥ 2. calculate_sofa() returns numbers per row, calculation one line.
code: per_subject <- scored[!duplicated(scored$subject_id),                       c("subject_id", "SOFA_admission", "SOFA_max")] per_subject$delta_SOFA <- per_subject$SOFA_max - per_subject$SOFA_admission  table(deteriorated_2plus = per_subject$delta_SOFA >= 2) #> deteriorated_2plus #> FALSE  TRUE  #>    12    48  hist(per_subject$delta_SOFA,      breaks = seq(-0.5, max(per_subject$delta_SOFA) + 0.5, by = 1),      col = "grey80", border = "white",      xlab = "SOFA_max - SOFA_admission",      main = "Per-patient change from admission to peak") abline(v = 2, lty = 2, col = "firebrick")

title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: Two quick sanity checks tying sub-scores back clinical inputs. respiratory sub-score higher ventilated rows; cardiovascular sub-score higher rows vasopressors.  shifts go expected direction — useful smoke test can copy onto data wiring column-name mapping.
code: op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) boxplot(SOFA_resp ~ ventilated, data = scored,         names = c("not vent.", "ventilated"),         ylab  = "Respiratory SOFA",         main  = "Resp. SOFA by ventilation") boxplot(SOFA_cardio ~ vasopressors, data = scored,         names = c("no pressors", "on pressors"),         ylab  = "Cardiovascular SOFA",         main  = "Cardio. SOFA by vasopressor use") par(op)

title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: Real datasets rarely come package’s default column names units. Override call site. example uses mmHg PaO2/FiO2 mg/dL bilirubin creatinine, custom column names throughout. variable unmeasured dataset, pass NULL . na_strategy = "sum_na_zero" (default) missing component contributes 0 total; na_strategy = "propagate" total becomes NA whenever sub-score missing.
code: my_data <- data.frame(   pid       = c("A", "B", "C"),   pf_ratio  = c(420, 220, 95),   is_vent   = c(0, 1, 1),   plt       = c(180, 60, 22),   bili_mgdl = c(0.7, 3.2, 8.0),   pressors  = c(0, 1, 1),   ne_dose   = c(0, 0.06, 0.25),   map_mmhg  = c(82, 68, 58),   on_sed    = c(0, 1, 1),   gcs       = c(15, NA, 5),   cr_mgdl   = c(0.9, 1.6, 3.4),   uo_24h    = c(2200, 900, 250),   rrt       = c(0, 0, 1) )  calculate_sofa(   my_data,   pao2_fio2     = "pf_ratio",   units_pao2_fio2  = "mmHg",   ventilated    = "is_vent",   platelets     = "plt",   bilirubin     = "bili_mgdl",  units_bilirubin  = "mg/dL",   vasopressors  = "pressors",   noradrenaline = "ne_dose",   map           = "map_mmhg",   sedation      = "on_sed",   gcs           = "gcs",   creatinine    = "cr_mgdl",    units_creatinine = "mg/dL",   urine_output  = "uo_24h",   dialysis      = "rrt" )[, c("pid", "SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",       "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal", "SOFA_score")] #>   pid SOFA_resp SOFA_coag SOFA_liver SOFA_cardio SOFA_neuro SOFA_renal #> 1   A         0         0          0           0          0          0 #> 2   B         2         2          2           3          2          1 #> 3   C         4         3          3           4          4          4 #>   SOFA_score #> 1          0 #> 2         12 #> 3         22 no_urine <- my_data no_urine$uo_24h <- NA  calculate_sofa(no_urine,                pao2_fio2     = "pf_ratio",   units_pao2_fio2  = "mmHg",                ventilated    = "is_vent",                platelets     = "plt",                bilirubin     = "bili_mgdl",  units_bilirubin  = "mg/dL",                vasopressors  = "pressors",                noradrenaline = "ne_dose",                map           = "map_mmhg",                sedation      = "on_sed",                creatinine    = "cr_mgdl",    units_creatinine = "mg/dL",                urine_output  = "uo_24h",                dialysis      = "rrt",                na_strategy   = "propagate")[, c("pid", "SOFA_score")] #>   pid SOFA_score #> 1   A          0 #> 2   B         12 #> 3   C         22

title: Case study: SOFA in a synthetic ICU cohort
text: cohort used synthetic. Distributions loosely calibrated look plausible adult ICU service, data suitable clinical inference — exist demonstrate package API shape analyses enables. Replicate notebook dataset patterns tell something meaningful; replicate example_sofa_data() patterns tell generator.

title: Edge cases and limits
text: SOFA thresholds integers (near-integers) original paper, real measurements floating point. boundaries package symmetric — half-open (>= lower, < upper), closed-closed. Two cases worth knowing .

title: Edge cases and limits
text: creatinine rules written endpoints inclusive: works perfectly integers, creatinine value lands tiers (e.g., 170.5 µmol/L) matches rule falls NA: real datasets typically appears unit conversion mg/dL, multiplies 88.4 produces non-integer values: 1.93 mg/dL × 88.4 = 170.6 µmol/L falls 170↔︎171 gap returns NA. Workaround: round creatinine nearest whole µmol/L scoring, work natively µmol/L:
code: < 110                       -> 0 >= 110 & <= 170             -> 1 >= 171 & <= 299             -> 2 >= 300 & <= 440             -> 3 > 440                       -> 4 score_renal(c(170, 170.5, 171, 299, 299.5, 300)) #> [1]  1 NA  2  2 NA  3 # Two patients with creatinine values that differ by 0.01 mg/dL score_renal(c(1.92, 1.93, 1.94), units = "mg/dL") #> [1]  1 NA  2 cr_mgdl <- c(1.92, 1.93, 1.94) score_renal(round(cr_mgdl * 88.4), units = "umol/L") #> [1] 1 2 2

what: Liver bilirubin tier 3 is closed at the top
title: Edge cases and limits
text: thresholds package half-open, liver tier-3 upper bound inclusive: 204 µmol/L tier 3, 204.001 tier 4. rounding awareness applies converting mg/dL (× 17.1):

what: 2. Unit conversion can flip a tier
title: Edge cases and limits
text: respiratory rule uses rounded kPa thresholds (13.3, 26.7, 40, 53.3) canonical mmHg cut-points (100, 200, 300, 400). agree within ~0.1 kPa, value sitting line mmHg can land side line kPa. mmHg, 99.9 100 → tier 4. kPa, 13.32 13.3 → tier 3. Pick unit stick . Don’t round-trip values mmHg → kPa → mmHg scoring purposes.
code: # 99.9 mmHg = 13.32 kPa — same physiological state, two units score_respiratory(99.9,  ventilated = 1, units = "mmHg") #> [1] 4 score_respiratory(13.32, ventilated = 1, units = "kPa") #> [1] 3

what: 3. The ventilation cap requires an explicit FALSE
title: Edge cases and limits
text: Sub-scores 3 4 respiratory subscale reserved mechanically ventilated patients. package implements : “ventilated supplied FALSE, demote 3/4 2.” demotion fires explicit FALSE — NA NULL ventilation leave high score place. intentional — silently demoting NA-ventilation rows mean quietly -scoring patients whose ventilation status simply wasn’t recorded. mean NA ventilated column inflates respiratory subscore relative column missing means “”. dataset uses NA mean “ventilator”, recode FALSE/0 first.
code: pf <- 80   # severely hypoxic (mmHg)  # Each row uses a different ventilation signal score_respiratory(pf, ventilated = TRUE,  units = "mmHg")  # 4 — vented #> [1] 4 score_respiratory(pf, ventilated = FALSE, units = "mmHg")  # 2 — capped #> [1] 2 score_respiratory(pf, ventilated = NA,    units = "mmHg")  # 4 — NOT capped #> [1] 4 score_respiratory(pf, ventilated = NULL,  units = "mmHg")  # 4 — NOT capped #> [1] 4

what: 4. The vasopressor flag is a companion, not a substitute, for dose
title: Edge cases and limits
text: score_cardiovascular() accepts two calling conventions: detailed dosing — pass dopamine, dobutamine, norepinephrine, epinephrine doses µg/kg/min; simple flag — pass vasopressors = TRUE/FALSE together numeric norepinephrine dose. flag escalate score — modulates norepinephrine dose interpreted. patient vasopressors = 1 recorded dose stays MAP-tiers (0 1): get tier 3 4 need actual dose: dataset binary “pressors” flag doses, recover tiers 3–4 alone. ’s package limitation — ’s SOFA limitation. Decide explicitly whether record default dose, treat rows tier 2, leave MAP tiers document choice.
code: # Three patients with hypotension on pressors but no numeric dose score_cardiovascular(map = c(60, 60, 60),                      vasopressors = c(1, 1, 1)) #> [1] 1 1 1 score_cardiovascular(map = c(60, 60, 60),                      vasopressors   = c(1, 1, 1),                      norepinephrine = c(0.05, 0.15, 0.30)) #> [1] 3 4 4

title: Edge cases and limits
text: calculate_sofa() aggregates six sub-scores total. default na_strategy = "sum_na_zero" treats missing components 0, total sum(non-missing parts). convenient one variable unmeasured — can hide pervasive missingness: default makes sense partially observed rows; sparsely observed rows misleading. Two defenses: Track per-row coverage explicitly reject rows threshold. Use na_strategy = "propagate" ’d rather see NA spuriously low total.
code: # A "patient" with only platelets recorded sparse <- data.frame(   pao2_fio2  = NA_real_,   ventilated = NA,   platelets  = 30,           # tier 3   bilirubin  = NA_real_,   vasopressors = NA,   noradrenaline = NA_real_,   map        = NA_real_,   sedation   = NA,   gcs        = NA_real_,   creatinine = NA_real_,   urine_output = NA_real_,   dialysis   = NA )  calculate_sofa(sparse)$SOFA_score          # default: 3 (looks low) #> [1] 3 calculate_sofa(sparse,                na_strategy = "propagate")$SOFA_score   # honest: NA #> [1] NA sub <- c("SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",          "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal")  scored <- calculate_sofa(sparse) n_observed <- rowSums(!is.na(scored[, sub])) data.frame(SOFA_score = scored$SOFA_score,            n_observed = n_observed) #>   SOFA_score n_observed #> 1          3          1

what: 6. Out-of-range GCS becomes NA, not an error
title: Edge cases and limits
text: neurological rule covers GCS 3–15 (clinical range), < 6 tier 4 == 15 tier 0. Values outside clinical range (typos, decimals, miscoded zeros) match rule silently return NA: Note GCS = 0 match < 6 scores tier 4 — ’s floor check. real-world failure mode: CSV exporter writes “0” “assessed” → every row becomes max-severity neurological score. Validate GCS 3–15 range scoring, decide explicitly whether --range values NA, error, clamped.
code: # 15 is correct; 115 is a typo; 0 is a miscoded missing score_neurological(c(15, 115, 0, 7.5, NA)) #> [1]  0 NA  4  3 NA clean_gcs <- function(x) {   x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))   x[x < 3 | x > 15] <- NA   x }  score_neurological(clean_gcs(c(15, 115, 0, 7.5, NA))) #> [1]  0 NA NA  3 NA

title: Edge cases and limits
text: SOFA_max computed max(..., na.rm = TRUE). subject every SOFA_score value missing — possible na_strategy = "propagate" plus pervasive missingness — base R’s max() returns -Inf warning: -Inf mathematically correct (“max nothing”) rarely want downstream analysis. Filter subjects calling calculate_sofa(), post-process SOFA_max NA equals -Inf.
code: # A subject with no measurements anywhere on either day ghost <- data.frame(   subject_id   = c(1, 1),   day          = c(0, 1),   pao2_fio2    = NA_real_, ventilated   = NA,   platelets    = NA_real_, bilirubin    = NA_real_,   vasopressors = NA,       noradrenaline = NA_real_,   map          = NA_real_, sedation     = NA,   gcs          = NA_real_,   creatinine   = NA_real_, urine_output = NA_real_, dialysis = NA )  suppressWarnings(   calculate_sofa(ghost,                  id   = "subject_id",                  time = "day",                  na_strategy = "propagate") )[, c("subject_id", "day", "SOFA_score", "SOFA_admission", "SOFA_max")] #>   subject_id day SOFA_score SOFA_admission SOFA_max #> 1          1   0         NA             NA     -Inf #> 2          1   1         NA             NA     -Inf

title: Edge cases and limits
text: Putting lessons together, ’s small wrapper several teams found useful: deliberately small — point wrap package, show gotcha one two lines defend know exists.
code: score_with_guards <- function(df, ...,                               gcs_col       = "gcs",                               vent_col      = "ventilated",                               min_observed  = 4) {   # 1. Range-check GCS — typos/zeros become NA, not tier 4   if (gcs_col %in% names(df)) {     g <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[gcs_col]]))     g[g < 3 | g > 15] <- NA     df[[gcs_col]] <- g   }   # 2. NA ventilation -> FALSE so the cap fires conservatively   if (vent_col %in% names(df)) {     v <- df[[vent_col]]     v[is.na(v)] <- 0     df[[vent_col]] <- v   }   # 3. Score with propagate so missingness is visible   out <- calculate_sofa(df, ..., na_strategy = "propagate")   # 4. Compute coverage and clear scores below the threshold   sub <- c("SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",            "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal")   n_obs <- rowSums(!is.na(out[, sub]))   out$SOFA_score[n_obs < min_observed] <- NA   out$n_observed <- n_obs   out }

title: Edge cases and limits
text: Vincent, J. L., Moreno, R., Takala, J., Willatts, S., De Mendonça, ., Bruining, H., … Thijs, L. G. (1996). SOFA (Sepsis-related Organ Failure Assessment) score describe organ dysfunction/failure. Intensive Care Medicine, 22(7), 707–710. https://doi.org/10.1007/BF01709751

what: Does this match the original Vincent 1996 SOFA?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: Yes. Thresholds tier definitions follow original publication (Vincent et al., 1996, Intensive Care Medicine 22(7), 707–710). paper specifies half-open interval (e.g., >= 110, <= 170 µmol/L renal tier 1), implementation reproduces exactly — including small consequences described Edge cases limits.

what: Is this validated for clinical use?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: . sofamatic research-grade implementation published scoring rule. scoring logic unit-tested boundary conditions (tests/testthat/), package regulatory clearance medical device. Clinical interpretation, validation local cohort, downstream use user’s responsibility.

what: Can I use it on MIMIC, eICU, or AmsterdamUMCdb?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: Yes. package ship third-party dataset, calculate_sofa() accepts arbitrary column names slots existing extracts without renaming columns: See case study full worked example using package’s synthetic data, mirrors layout typical cohorts.
code: calculate_sofa(   mimic_extract,   pao2_fio2     = "pf_ratio",     units_pao2_fio2  = "mmHg",   bilirubin     = "bilirubin_mg", units_bilirubin  = "mg/dL",   creatinine    = "creat_mg",     units_creatinine = "mg/dL",   noradrenaline = "norepi_dose",   id            = "stay_id",   time          = "icu_day" )

what: What about qSOFA, mSOFA, or pediatric SOFA?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: scope sofamatic. package implements canonical adult SOFA . Variants different thresholds (qSOFA), reduced component sets (mSOFA), different age-banded normal ranges (pediatric / pSOFA) deserve implementations clinical validation; mixing single function invite errors.

what: How does the package handle multiple measurements per day?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: doesn’t aggregate — row scored independently. raw data contains several measurements per patient per day, aggregation upstream decide rule applies (worst--day common):
code: # Worst-of-day reduction before scoring daily <- aggregate(   cbind(pao2_fio2, platelets, bilirubin, map, gcs, creatinine) ~     subject_id + day,   data = raw,   FUN  = function(x) min(x, na.rm = TRUE)   # worst MAP/GCS/PF; max for cr/bili )

what: Why is SOFA_admission constant within subject?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: ’s SOFA_score earliest time per id, broadcast back every row subject. Storing every row means can compute delta_SOFA <- SOFA_max - SOFA_admission without extra join merge(). applies SOFA_max.

what: Can I use it with tidyverse / data.table?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: Yes — calculate_sofa() just function takes returns data.frame, composes native pipe ecosystems:
code: library(dplyr) icu |>   calculate_sofa(id = "subject_id", time = "day") |>   group_by(subject_id) |>   summarise(delta = first(SOFA_max) - first(SOFA_admission)) library(data.table) setDT(calculate_sofa(icu, id = "subject_id", time = "day"))[   , .(delta = first(SOFA_max) - first(SOFA_admission)), by = subject_id]

what: How large a dataset can it handle?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: component function vectorised input vectors, cost scales linearly row count dominated underlying arithmetic, R-level overhead. Million-row inputs run seconds laptop. datasets don’t fit memory, partition upstream call calculate_sofa() per chunk — function stateless except optional id/time aggregation, perform whole-cohort output.

what: Why is SOFA_score non-NA even when most inputs are missing?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: default na_strategy = "sum_na_zero" treats missing sub-scores 0 total. convenient partially observed rows hides pervasive missingness. Switch na_strategy = "propagate" ’d rather see NA whenever sub-score missing — see Edge cases limits worked example defensive wrapper.

what: Why does my non-ventilated patient still get respiratory tier 4?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: Tiers 3 4 respiratory sub-score require ventilation. demotion tier 2 fires ventilated explicitly FALSE/0 — NA NULL leave higher tier place, silently demoting unknown rows systematically -score patients missing ventilation status. dataset encodes “ventilator” NA, recode FALSE/0 scoring.

what: Why does a vasopressors = TRUE flag with no dose stay at MAP tiers?
title: Frequently asked questions
text: flag companion numeric norepinephrine dose, substitute . SOFA cardiovascular tiers 2–4 defined specific drug-dose thresholds; binary “pressors” flag contain enough information assign one. flag dose, recover tiers 3–4 alone — limitation score definition, package.

title: Frequently asked questions
text: tests/testthat/. Run devtools::test() testthat::test_local() package root. Coverage reported Codecov every push main.

title: Frequently asked questions
text: R >= 4.0.0. package depends base R stats — runtime dependencies.

title: Frequently asked questions
text: repository also includes CITATION.cff file, GitHub uses render Cite repository button project page.
code: citation("sofamatic")

title: Introduction to Sofamatic
text: sofamatic computes Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) score six sub-scores clinical data. exposes one vectorised function per component plus calculate_sofa() aggregator takes long-format data frame returns augmented sub-scores, total score, (optionally) longitudinal helpers (SOFA_admission, SOFA_max). thresholds follow canonical definition (Vincent et al., 1996). SI units (kPa, µmol/L) conventional units (mmHg, mg/dL) supported.
code: library(sofamatic)

title: Introduction to Sofamatic
text: component vectorised function: Use ?score_respiratory (etc.) see full thresholds unit conventions.
code: score_respiratory(c(450, 350, 250, 150, 80),                   ventilated = c(0, 0, 1, 1, 1),                   units = "mmHg") #> [1] 0 1 2 3 4  score_coagulation(c(180, 130, 75, 35, 12)) #> [1] 0 1 2 3 4  score_liver(c(0.8, 1.5, 3, 8, 14), units = "mg/dL") #> [1] 0 1 2 3 4  score_cardiovascular(map = c(80, 65, 60, 60),                      norepinephrine = c(0, 0, 0.05, 0.2)) #> [1] 0 1 3 4  score_neurological(c(15, 13, 11, 8, 4)) #> [1] 0 1 2 3 4  score_renal(creatinine = c(0.9, 1.5, 2.5, 4, 6), units = "mg/dL") #> [1] 0 1 2 3 4

title: Introduction to Sofamatic
text: calculate_sofa() accepts data frame column names. Defaults match parameter names (pao2_fio2, platelets, bilirubin, map, gcs, creatinine, …); every column name overridable.
code: df <- example_sofa_data(n_subjects = 4, days_each = 1) calculate_sofa(df)[, c("SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",                        "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal",                        "SOFA_score")] #>   SOFA_resp SOFA_coag SOFA_liver SOFA_cardio SOFA_neuro SOFA_renal SOFA_score #> 1         2         1          1           0          3          4         11 #> 2         2         1          0           3          2          4         12 #> 3         1         4          2           3          2          0         12 #> 4         2         0          0           3          4          2         11

title: Introduction to Sofamatic
text: long-format longitudinal data (one row per subject per day), supply id time also get SOFA_admission (first day) SOFA_max (worst day) per subject.
code: df  <- example_sofa_data(n_subjects = 3, days_each = 4) out <- calculate_sofa(df, id = "subject_id", time = "day") out[, c("subject_id", "day",         "SOFA_score", "SOFA_admission", "SOFA_max")] #>    subject_id day SOFA_score SOFA_admission SOFA_max #> 1           1   0          3              3        9 #> 2           1   1          7              3        9 #> 3           1   2          5              3        9 #> 4           1   3          9              3        9 #> 5           2   0          5              5        9 #> 6           2   1          7              5        9 #> 7           2   2          8              5        9 #> 8           2   3          9              5        9 #> 9           3   0          8              8       12 #> 10          3   1          8              8       12 #> 11          3   2          9              8       12 #> 12          3   3         12              8       12

title: Introduction to Sofamatic
text: calculate_sofa(na_strategy = "sum_na_zero") (default) treats NA sub-scores 0 total — convenient components unmeasured. Use na_strategy = "propagate" instead return NA whenever sub-score missing. neurological sub-score, score_neurological() accepts sedation flag. missing GCS non-sedated patient treated “alert” (score 0); missing GCS sedated patient treated moderate impairment (score 2). reflects common ICU practice fully responsive, non-sedated patients often re-assessed, leaving GCS field blank.

title: Introduction to Sofamatic
text: example_sofa_data() returns fully synthetic, longitudinal ICU-style dataset. data simulated never describe real patient. Distributions loosely calibrated look plausible (e.g., lower PaO2/FiO2 ventilated rows, occasional missing GCS sedated rows, ~10 % dialysis prevalence), never used draw clinical conclusions.
code: str(example_sofa_data(n_subjects = 2, days_each = 2)) #> 'data.frame':    4 obs. of  17 variables: #>  $ subject_id   : int  1 1 2 2 #>  $ day          : int  0 1 0 1 #>  $ age          : num  78 78 55 55 #>  $ sex          : chr  "F" "F" "M" "M" #>  $ bmi          : num  30.2 30.2 29 29 #>  $ pao2_fio2    : num  24.5 35.4 55.1 13.9 #>  $ ventilated   : num  1 1 0 1 #>  $ platelets    : num  229 252 137 215 #>  $ bilirubin    : num  2 5 4 2 #>  $ vasopressors : num  0 1 0 0 #>  $ noradrenaline: num  0 0.045 0 0 #>  $ map          : num  92.1 89 75.7 77.4 #>  $ sedation     : num  1 0 0 1 #>  $ gcs          : int  10 4 5 10 #>  $ creatinine   : num  112 122 88 155 #>  $ urine_output : num  1064 679 1760 1013 #>  $ dialysis     : num  1 0 0 0

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: Automatic SOFA scoring R. Sofamatic small, dependency-light R package computes Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) score six sub-scores clinical data. works cross-sectional snapshots longitudinal (one-row-per-day) layouts, supports SI well conventional units. Thresholds follow Vincent et al. (1996).

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: Six vectorised component scorers (score_respiratory(), score_coagulation(), score_liver(), score_cardiovascular(), score_neurological(), score_renal()) plus single-call aggregator calculate_sofa(). Cross-sectional longitudinal layouts; longitudinal mode adds SOFA_admission SOFA_max per subject call. SI conventional units PaO2/FiO2, bilirubin, creatinine. Configurable handling missing GCS sedated vs non-sedated patients, two strategies aggregating missing sub-scores. Base-R runtime — extra dependencies regulated air-gapped environments.

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: package yet CRAN. Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ksa98/sofamatic")

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
code: library(sofamatic)  df  <- example_sofa_data(n_subjects = 5, days_each = 4) out <- calculate_sofa(df, id = "subject_id", time = "day")  head(out[, c("subject_id", "day",              "SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",              "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal",              "SOFA_score", "SOFA_admission", "SOFA_max")])

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: Every component returns integer vector {0, 1, 2, 3, 4} (NA). calculate_sofa() returns input data frame augmented one column per sub-score, total SOFA_score, (id/time supplied) SOFA_admission SOFA_max per subject.

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
code: # Cross-sectional: one row per patient calculate_sofa(df_xs)  # Longitudinal: one row per (patient, day) calculate_sofa(df_long, id = "subject_id", time = "day")

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: Defaults match parameter names (pao2_fio2, platelets, bilirubin, map, gcs, creatinine, urine_output, …). Override match dataset: Pass NULL optional column don’t . example, dataset urine output dialysis flag, renal sub-score derived creatinine alone:
code: calculate_sofa(   df,   pao2_fio2 = "pf_ratio",   platelets = "plt",   bilirubin = "tot_bili_mgdl",   units_bilirubin  = "mg/dL",   units_creatinine = "mg/dL",   units_pao2_fio2  = "mmHg" ) calculate_sofa(df, urine_output = NULL, dialysis = NULL)

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: Two policies total score: na_strategy = "sum_na_zero" (default) — missing sub-scores contribute 0 total. Useful components unmeasured. na_strategy = "propagate" — missing sub-score yields NA total. neurological sub-score, score_neurological() accepts sedation flag. missing GCS non-sedated patient treated “alert” (score 0); missing GCS sedated patient treated moderate impairment (score 2). Override na_strategy want different behaviour.

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: example_sofa_data(n_subjects, days_each, seed) returns longitudinal, ICU-style data frame one row per (subject, day) columns expected calculate_sofa(). Values drawn simple parametric generator: Per-subject statics drawn broadcast subject’s days: age ~ Normal(62, 12), sex sampled P(M) = 0.6, bmi ~ Normal(27, 5). Per-row flags independent Bernoullis: ventilation P = 0.85, sedation P = 0.55, vasopressor use P = 0.40, dialysis P = 0.08. Conditional measurements depend flags. PaO2/FiO2 (kPa) drawn Normal(25, 8) ventilated rows Normal(50, 8) otherwise (floored 5). MAP drawn Normal(70, 10) vasopressors Normal(80, 10) . Norepinephrine dose Normal(0.06, 0.05) µg/kg/min vasopressor rows (floored 0.001) exactly 0 elsewhere. Independent measurements use floored Normals: platelets Normal(180, 70), bilirubin Normal(25, 25), creatinine Normal(110, 60), urine output Normal(1500, 600). GCS sampled uniformly 3–15, roughly quarter sedated rows blanked NA mirror real-world pattern un-recorded GCS fully sedated patients. seed argument exposed (default 42) output reproducible. generator deliberately simple: distributions independent, day--day values autocorrelated, parameters illustrative rather derived specific cohort. Use data examples, tests, benchmarks — clinical inference.

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: Full reference articles published https://ksa98.github.io/sofamatic/: Introduction — package tour, single-component scoring, longitudinal mode. Case study: SOFA synthetic ICU cohort — worked applied analysis raw data deterioration metrics. Edge cases limits — boundary arithmetic, unit-conversion gotchas, missing-data policies, defensive scoring wrapper. FAQ — short answers recurring questions.

title: Sofamatic — SOFA scoring in R
text: Bug reports, feature requests, pull requests welcome. Please read CONTRIBUTING.md workflow, open issues https://github.com/ksa98/sofamatic/issues. Issue templates bugs feature requests provided.

what: Calculate SOFA scores for a clinical dataset — calculate_sofa
title: Calculate SOFA scores for a clinical dataset — calculate_sofa
text: calculate_sofa() workhorse package. takes data frame (cross-sectional longitudinal long format) appends six SOFA subscores plus total SOFA_score column. longitudinal data (one row per subject per day), supply id optionally time also get SOFA_admission (first observation) SOFA_max (worst score follow-) per subject.

title: Calculate SOFA scores for a clinical dataset — calculate_sofa
code: calculate_sofa(   data,   pao2_fio2 = "pao2_fio2",   ventilated = "ventilated",   platelets = "platelets",   bilirubin = "bilirubin",   vasopressors = "vasopressors",   noradrenaline = "noradrenaline",   dopamine = NULL,   dobutamine = NULL,   epinephrine = NULL,   map = "map",   sedation = "sedation",   gcs = "gcs",   creatinine = "creatinine",   urine_output = "urine_output",   dialysis = "dialysis",   id = NULL,   time = NULL,   units_pao2_fio2 = c("kPa", "mmHg"),   units_bilirubin = c("umol/L", "mg/dL"),   units_creatinine = c("umol/L", "mg/dL"),   na_strategy = c("sum_na_zero", "propagate") )

title: Calculate SOFA scores for a clinical dataset — calculate_sofa
text: data data frame long format. row one observation (one subject, one time point). pao2_fio2, platelets, bilirubin, map, gcs, creatinine Column names (character strings) holding corresponding clinical measurements. ventilated, sedation, vasopressors, dialysis Optional column names binary indicators (logical 0/1). Pass NULL skip variable. noradrenaline, dopamine, dobutamine, epinephrine Optional column names vasoactive drug doses (ug/kg/min). Pass NULL skip drug. urine_output Optional column name 24-hour urine output (mL). id, time Optional column names subject id time. supplied, returned data frame includes SOFA_admission (SOFA_score earliest time per id) SOFA_max (maximum SOFA_score per id). units_pao2_fio2 "kPa" "mmHg". units_bilirubin, units_creatinine "umol/L" "mg/dL". na_strategy aggregate six subscores total. One "sum_na_zero" (default – NA subscores contribute 0 total, matching rowSums(..., na.rm = TRUE)) "propagate" (NA subscore yields NA total).

title: Calculate SOFA scores for a clinical dataset — calculate_sofa
text: data frame: data augmented columns SOFA_resp, SOFA_coag, SOFA_liver, SOFA_cardio, SOFA_neuro, SOFA_renal, SOFA_score, plus SOFA_admission SOFA_max id/time supplied.

title: Calculate SOFA scores for a clinical dataset — calculate_sofa
text: Default column names match parameter names data frame columns pao2_fio2, platelets, bilirubin, map, gcs, creatinine, etc. works box. Override datasets use different naming conventions.

title: Calculate SOFA scores for a clinical dataset — calculate_sofa
code: df <- example_sofa_data() out <- calculate_sofa(df, id = "subject_id", time = "day") head(out[, c("subject_id", "day",              "SOFA_resp", "SOFA_coag", "SOFA_liver",              "SOFA_cardio", "SOFA_neuro", "SOFA_renal",              "SOFA_score", "SOFA_admission", "SOFA_max")]) #>   subject_id day SOFA_resp SOFA_coag SOFA_liver SOFA_cardio SOFA_neuro #> 1          1   0         2         1          0           1          2 #> 2          1   1         2         0          0           0          3 #> 3          1   2         2         0          1           1          3 #> 4          1   3         3         0          2           0          2 #> 5          1   4         3         0          2           3          1 #> 6          2   0         2         2          0           1          1 #>   SOFA_renal SOFA_score SOFA_admission SOFA_max #> 1          1          7              7       13 #> 2          0          5              7       13 #> 3          2          9              7       13 #> 4          1          8              7       13 #> 5          4         13              7       13 #> 6          0          6              6       11

title: Synthetic ICU example data — example_sofa_data
text: Returns synthetic, longitudinal data set layout typical ICU case-report form: one row per (subject, day) columns needed calculate_sofa() plus demographic / context columns.

title: Synthetic ICU example data — example_sofa_data
code: example_sofa_data(n_subjects = 10, days_each = 5, seed = 42)

title: Synthetic ICU example data — example_sofa_data
text: n_subjects Number subjects. days_each Number consecutive observation days per subject. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility (default 42).

title: Synthetic ICU example data — example_sofa_data
text: data frame n_subjects * days_each rows following columns: subject_id integer subject id day 0-indexed day since admission age years sex "F"/"M" bmi kg/m^2 pao2_fio2 kPa ventilated 0/1 platelets 10^9/L bilirubin umol/L vasopressors 0/1 flag noradrenaline ug/kg/min map mmHg sedation 0/1 gcs 3-15 (occasional NA sedated patients) creatinine umol/L urine_output mL/24h dialysis 0/1

title: Synthetic ICU example data — example_sofa_data
text: data entirely simulated contain patient information. Distributions loosely calibrated plausible adult ICU service (e.g., pao2_fio2 skewing low ventilated patients, occasional missing gcs sedated patients, ~10 % dialysis prevalence) never used draw clinical conclusions.

what: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
title: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
text: Scores cardiovascular subscale SOFA mean arterial pressure (MAP) , available, dosing vasoactive drugs. function supports two calling conventions:

title: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
code: score_cardiovascular(   map,   vasopressors = NULL,   dopamine = NULL,   dobutamine = NULL,   norepinephrine = NULL,   epinephrine = NULL )

title: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
text: map Numeric vector mean arterial pressure mmHg. vasopressors Optional logical 0/1 numeric flag indicating vasopressor running. dopamine, dobutamine, norepinephrine, epinephrine Optional numeric vectors drug dose ug/kg/min. NULL (default) means drug given data unavailable.

title: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
text: Integer vector {0, 1, 2, 3, 4} (NA_integer_).

title: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
text: Detailed dosing – pass subset dopamine, dobutamine, norepinephrine, epinephrine numeric vectors (ug/kg/min). follows canonical SOFA definition (Vincent 1996) recommended path individual drug doses recorded. Simple flag + norepinephrine dose – pass vasopressors (logical 0/1) together norepinephrine (ug/kg/min). convenient norepinephrine dose recorded numerically vasoactive use captured single binary flag. neither vasoactive information supplied, score determined MAP alone (0 1).

title: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
text: 4: dopamine > 15, norepinephrine > 0.1, epinephrine > 0.1 3: dopamine > 5, norepinephrine > 0 (<= 0.1), epinephrine > 0 (<= 0.1) 2: dopamine > 0 (<= 5), dobutamine 1: MAP < 70 mmHg vasoactive support 0: MAP >= 70 mmHg vasoactive support

title: Cardiovascular component of the SOFA score — score_cardiovascular
code: # detailed dosing score_cardiovascular(map = c(80, 65, 60, 60),                      norepinephrine = c(0, 0, 0.05, 0.2)) #> [1] 0 1 3 4  # simple-flag style: vasopressors as a binary indicator score_cardiovascular(map = c(80, 65, 60, 60),                      vasopressors = c(0, 0, 1, 1),                      norepinephrine = c(0, 0, 0.05, 0.2)) #> [1] 0 1 3 4

what: Coagulation component of the SOFA score — score_coagulation
title: Coagulation component of the SOFA score — score_coagulation
text: Scores coagulation subscale SOFA platelet count.

title: Coagulation component of the SOFA score — score_coagulation
code: score_coagulation(platelets)

title: Coagulation component of the SOFA score — score_coagulation
text: platelets Numeric vector platelet counts 10^9 / L (equivalent 1000 / mm^3).

title: Coagulation component of the SOFA score — score_coagulation
text: Integer vector {0, 1, 2, 3, 4} (NA_integer_).

title: Coagulation component of the SOFA score — score_coagulation
Coagulation component of the SOFA score — score_coagulation

what: Hepatic component of the SOFA score — score_liver
title: Hepatic component of the SOFA score — score_liver
text: Scores liver subscale SOFA total serum bilirubin.

title: Hepatic component of the SOFA score — score_liver
code: score_liver(bilirubin, units = c("umol/L", "mg/dL"))

title: Hepatic component of the SOFA score — score_liver
text: bilirubin Numeric vector total bilirubin. units One "umol/L" (default) "mg/dL". Conversion factor 1 mg/dL ~ 17.1 umol/L.

title: Hepatic component of the SOFA score — score_liver
text: Integer vector {0, 1, 2, 3, 4} (NA_integer_).

title: Hepatic component of the SOFA score — score_liver
Hepatic component of the SOFA score — score_liver

what: Neurological component of the SOFA score — score_neurological
title: Neurological component of the SOFA score — score_neurological
text: Scores neurological subscale SOFA Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS). sedation supplied, missing GCS sedated patient interpreted moderate impairment (score 2), whereas missing GCS non-sedated patient treated best clinical state (score 0). reflects common ICU practice fully responsive, non-sedated patient often re-assessed GCS field left blank.

title: Neurological component of the SOFA score — score_neurological
code: score_neurological(gcs, sedation = NULL, na_strategy = c("na", "zero"))

title: Neurological component of the SOFA score — score_neurological
text: gcs Integer numeric vector GCS values (3-15). sedation Optional logical 0/1 vector indicating whether patient sedated time assessment. NULL, missing GCS values produce NA_integer_ scores. na_strategy handle NA GCS sedation NULL. One "na" (default – return NA_integer_) "zero" (treat missing GCS best state, score 0).

title: Neurological component of the SOFA score — score_neurological
text: Integer vector {0, 1, 2, 3, 4} (NA_integer_).

title: Neurological component of the SOFA score — score_neurological
Neurological component of the SOFA score — score_neurological
code: score_neurological(c(15, 13, 11, 8, 4, NA)) #> [1]  0  1  2  3  4 NA score_neurological(c(15, 13, NA, NA),                    sedation = c(0, 0, 1, 0)) #> [1] 0 1 2 0

what: Renal component of the SOFA score — score_renal
title: Renal component of the SOFA score — score_renal
text: Scores renal subscale SOFA serum creatinine , optionally, 24-hour urine output. dialysis supplied, patient receiving dialysis assigned score 4.

title: Renal component of the SOFA score — score_renal
code: score_renal(   creatinine,   urine_output = NULL,   dialysis = NULL,   units = c("umol/L", "mg/dL") )

title: Renal component of the SOFA score — score_renal
text: creatinine Numeric vector serum creatinine. urine_output Optional numeric vector 24-hour urine output mL. dialysis Optional logical 0/1 vector indicating renal replacement therapy. Patients receiving dialysis scored 4 regardless creatinine urine output. units One "umol/L" (default) "mg/dL" creatinine. Conversion factor 1 mg/dL ~ 88.4 umol/L.

title: Renal component of the SOFA score — score_renal
text: Integer vector {0, 1, 2, 3, 4} (NA_integer_).

title: Renal component of the SOFA score — score_renal
Renal component of the SOFA score — score_renal
code: score_renal(c(80, 130, 200, 350, 500)) #> [1] 0 1 2 3 4 score_renal(creatinine = c(80, 130, 200),             urine_output = c(2000, 600, 150)) #> [1] 0 1 4 score_renal(creatinine = c(80, 80, 80),             dialysis = c(0, 0, 1)) #> [1] 0 0 4

what: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
title: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
text: Scores respiratory subscale Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) ratio arterial oxygen partial pressure fractional inspired oxygen (PaO2/FiO2). Sub-scores 3 4 assigned mechanically ventilated patients; ventilated supplied FALSE, score capped 2 (matching original SOFA definition).

title: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
code: score_respiratory(pao2_fio2, ventilated = NULL, units = c("kPa", "mmHg"))

title: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
text: pao2_fio2 Numeric vector PaO2/FiO2 ratios. ventilated Optional logical 0/1 numeric vector indicating mechanical ventilation. NULL (default), cap applied. units One "kPa" "mmHg". Defaults "kPa" (SI unit). Internally thresholds 100, 200, 300, 400 mmHg (~ 13.3, 26.7, 40, 53.3 kPa).

title: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
text: Integer vector {0, 1, 2, 3, 4} (NA_integer_) length pao2_fio2.

title: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
text: Using mmHg: PaO2/FiO2 < 100 ventilated: 4 PaO2/FiO2 < 200 ventilated: 3 PaO2/FiO2 < 300: 2 PaO2/FiO2 < 400: 1 PaO2/FiO2 >= 400: 0

title: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
text: Vincent, J. L., et al. (1996). SOFA (Sepsis-related Organ Failure Assessment) score describe organ dysfunction/failure. Intensive Care Medicine, 22(7), 707-710.

title: Respiratory component of the SOFA score — score_respiratory
code: score_respiratory(c(450, 350, 250, 150, 80),                   ventilated = c(0, 0, 1, 1, 1),                   units = "mmHg") #> [1] 0 1 2 3 4

title: Sofamatic: Sequential Organ Failure Assessment scoring in R — sofamatic-package
text: Automatic SOFA scoring R. Vectorised, dependency-light functions computing SOFA subscores total SOFA score clinical data, including longitudinal helpers first-day worst-day scoring.

title: Sofamatic: Sequential Organ Failure Assessment scoring in R — sofamatic-package
text: score_respiratory()    – PaO2/FiO2 optional ventilation flag score_coagulation()    – platelets score_liver()          – bilirubin score_cardiovascular() – MAP, vasoactive doses score_neurological()   – GCS, optional sedation score_renal()          – creatinine, urine output, dialysis

title: Sofamatic: Sequential Organ Failure Assessment scoring in R — sofamatic-package
text: calculate_sofa() takes data frame returns augmented six subscores, total SOFA_score, (longitudinal data) SOFA_admission SOFA_max per subject.

title: Sofamatic: Sequential Organ Failure Assessment scoring in R — sofamatic-package
text: use Sofamatic academic work, please cite package – see citation("sofamatic").

title: Sofamatic: Sequential Organ Failure Assessment scoring in R — sofamatic-package

title: Sofamatic 0.1.0
text: First public release. Sofar, good. score_respiratory(), score_coagulation(), score_liver(), score_cardiovascular(), score_neurological(), score_renal() — vectorised functions SOFA component, supporting SI conventional units applicable. calculate_sofa() — aggregate six sub-scores plus total SOFA_score long-format data frame, optional id/time arguments produce per-subject SOFA_admission (first day) SOFA_max (worst day). example_sofa_data() — synthetic longitudinal example documentation tests. Configurable handling missing GCS values via score_neurological() na_strategy calculate_sofa(). Full testthat suite (45+ tests covering boundary thresholds special-case logic vasoactive drugs, dialysis, missing GCS).
